prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `d017b73f`.
codex_response:
Rule: each nonzero component is a small 4-connected path fragment. Keep the fragments in left-to-right order, then translate each later fragment so its left endpoint sits exactly one cell to the right of the previous fragment’s right endpoint; render the packed chain on the same 3-row canvas and crop the width tightly.

The Sonnet summary was discarded as wrong. The Opus summary was partially useful but I corrected it in [notes.md](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_d017b73f/notes.md): the key invariant is endpoint-to-endpoint path chaining, not just generic “touching zig-zag packing.” No new reusable primitive was needed.

Verification passed end to end: official examples `5/5`, generated examples `1000/1000`. The generated family stays close to the source task: 3-row inputs, 3 or 4 separated path fragments, input widths `9` or `11`, and packed outputs of width `7`, `8`, or `9`, which matches the official distribution closely.
